one_liner: HubSpot offers a broad, self-serve REST API (CRM, marketing, sales) secured via OAuth2 or private app access tokens, with an official MCP server already available for AI agent integration.

auth_methods.notes: Required for multi-account/public/marketplace apps; Authorization Code grant, Bearer token in header.
Private app access tokens / static auth tokens used as Bearer tokens for single-account apps.

self_serve_notes: Free developer seats and private app tokens can be created directly in a HubSpot account without approval; free-tier rate limits apply (100 req/10s, 250k/day). OAuth apps for marketplace distribution require app creation but no gatekeeping beyond developer account signup.

api_surface.notes: Comprehensive CRM REST API (contacts, deals, engagements, associations, etc.) plus broader developer platform APIs across marketing, sales, service.

evidence.supports: Describes OAuth and private app access token authentication methods with Bearer header usage.
Details OAuth vs static auth token requirements for app distribution.
Free developer seat access for self-serve API usage.
Free-tier rate limits confirming self-serve free access.
Broad REST API surface across HubSpot platform.

flags: Official remote MCP server exists (HubSpot MCP Server) plus npm package @hubspot/mcp-server for local use, per search results though not directly cited in scraped docs.
Sensitive data properties excluded from MCP access per search summary.
